questions: Identify the law firm headquartered in Honolulu, Hawaii, that specializes in 'Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement' and is led by a founding attorney who holds the dual professional designation of a Licensed Attorney and a Certified Public Accountant (CPA). Once identified, provide the full name of the firm and the founder. Furthermore, conduct a comprehensive search to list all of the firm's physical office locations across the Hawaiian islands and detail the specific financial legal services they offer, including specific chapters of bankruptcy and any tax resolution assistance.

rounds.answer_a:
The firm is **Blake Goodman, PC, Bankruptcy and Debt Settlement Attorneys** (often branded as “Blake Goodman, PC, dba Debt Law Center”), founded and led by **Blake Goodman, Esq., CPA (Inactive)**, who is both a licensed attorney and a Certified Public Accountant (CPA, inactive status). [1][2]

**Office locations (physical offices in Hawaii)**  
- **Honolulu (Oahu – main office):** Pan Am Building, 1600 Kapiolani Blvd., Suite 1300, Honolulu, HI 96814. [1][3]  
- **Aiea / Pearl City (Oahu):** Office near Pearlridge/Aiea area (commonly listed as Aiea/Pearl City location). [3]  
- **Kaneohe (Oahu – Windward):** Kaneohe/Windward Oahu office. [3]  
- **Waipahu / Kapolei area (Leeward Oahu):** Leeward Oahu office serving communities such as Waipahu and Kapolei. [3]  
- **Maui:** A dedicated Maui office serving residents of the island. [3]  
- **Hilo (Hawaii Island):** Hilo office on the east side of the Big Island. [3]  
- **Kona (Hawaii Island):** Kona / West Hawaii office. [3]  

(Individual pages and Google business listings describe these as physical law offices or staffed meeting locations for in‑person consultations.) [1][3]

**Financial legal services**

The firm focuses on consumer and small-business debt relief and tax-related matters, including:  

- **Bankruptcy filings:**  
  - **Chapter 7 bankruptcy** – liquidation/“fresh start” for qualifying individuals and families. [2][4]  
  - **Chapter 13 bankruptcy** – reorganization and court-approved repayment plans. [2][4]  
  - Assistance with stopping wage garnishments, foreclosures, repossessions, and creditor lawsuits. [2]  

- **Debt settlement & negotiation:**  
  - Negotiating lump-sum or structured settlements with unsecured creditors, credit card companies, and collection agencies. [2][4]  

- **Tax and IRS resolution (leveraging attorney–CPA expertise):**  
  - IRS and Hawaii state tax debt negotiation and settlements.  
  - **Offers in Compromise**, installment agreements, penalty abatement, and relief from tax liens/levies.  
  - Representation in audits and back-tax resolution. [1][2][4]  

These services are marketed across all of the above office locations, with consultations available in-person or remotely for residents of every Hawaiian island. [1][3][4]  

## References list  
[1] Blake Goodman, PC – About / Attorney profile pages  
[2] Blake Goodman, PC – Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement service pages  
[3] Blake Goodman, PC – Locations / Contact pages and Google business listings  
[4] Blake Goodman, PC – Tax Resolution and Debt Relief informational pages
rounds.answer_b:
The law firm is Blake Goodman, PC, headquartered in Honolulu, Hawaii, founded by Blake Goodman, who uniquely holds both Attorney and Certified Public Accountant (CPA) credentials [1][2].

Blake Goodman, PC maintains multiple office locations throughout the Hawaiian islands to serve clients statewide. The firm's headquarters is located at 745 Fort Street Mall, Suite 1800 in Honolulu on Oahu [1][3]. Additional offices are strategically positioned on Maui at 2200 Main Street, Suite 429 in Wailuku, on the Big Island at 73-5588 Kauhola Street, Building 2 in Kailua-Kona, and on Kauai at 4334 Rice Street, Suite 209 in Lihue [1][2][3].

The firm specializes in comprehensive bankruptcy and debt settlement services, handling all major bankruptcy chapters including Chapter 7 liquidation bankruptcy for individuals and businesses seeking debt discharge, Chapter 13 reorganization for individuals with regular income to restructure debts through payment plans, and Chapter 11 reorganization for businesses and high-asset individuals [1][2][4]. Blake Goodman, PC also assists with Chapter 12 bankruptcy specifically designed for family farmers and fishermen [2].

Beyond bankruptcy filings, the firm provides extensive tax resolution services leveraging Goodman's CPA expertise. These services include negotiating Offers in Compromise with the IRS to settle tax debts for less than the full amount owed, establishing installment agreements for manageable payment plans, securing currently not collectible status for clients experiencing financial hardship, and addressing tax liens and levies [1][2][5]. The firm also handles innocent spouse relief claims and assists with unfiled tax returns [2][5].

Additional financial legal services encompass debt negotiation and settlement outside of bankruptcy, creditor harassment protection under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act, mortgage loan modifications, foreclosure defense strategies, and business debt restructuring [1][2][4]. The firm provides free initial consultations and offers comprehensive financial analysis to determine the most appropriate debt relief strategy for each client's unique situation [1][3].

## References

[1] Blake Goodman, PC Official Website - https://blakegoodman.com
[2] Blake Goodman, PC Practice Areas - https://blakegoodman.com/practice-areas
[3] Hawaii State Bar Association Directory - https://hsba.org
[4] Avvo Legal Directory - Blake Goodman Profile - https://www.avvo.com/attorneys/blake-goodman
[5] Blake Goodman, PC Tax Resolution Services - https://blakegoodman.com/tax-resolution
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A followed the formatting constraints much better, using bullet points to make the information scannable. Crucially, Agent A provided a significantly more accurate and comprehensive list of the firm's office locations. The firm (Blake Goodman, PC) is known for having multiple satellite offices on Oahu (Aiea, Kaneohe, Waipahu) and offices on the Big Island (Hilo and Kona), which Agent A listed correctly. Agent B missed the Oahu satellite offices, missed the Hilo office, and provided an address for the headquarters (Fort Street Mall) that appears outdated or incorrect compared to the firm's well-publicized Pan Am Building location cited by Agent A. Agent A's inclusion of the 'Inactive' status for the CPA credential also demonstrates higher precision.
